content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 23, 1993

William R. Galstan
City Attorney
City of Antioch
P.O. Box 130
Antioch, CA 94509-0504

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-93-103

Dear Mr. Galstan:

You have requested advice on behalf of the City of Antioch
concerning the lobbying disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Your letter states only a general
question. Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18239(c).2/

QUESTION

The City of Antioch (the "City") is a member of the League of
California Cities which engages in lobbying activities. Is it
necessary for the City to file lobbying disclosure reports?

CONCLUSION

The City will incur disclosure obligations if it 1) employs a
lobbyist or makes payments to a lobbying firm, or 2) makes
payments totaling $5,000 or more in a calendar quarter to
influence state legislative or administrative action. Regular
payments for membership in the League of California Cities need
not be counted for purposes of determining if the City must file
lobbying disclosure reports.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

William R. Galstan
Our File No. I-93-103
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Act requires disclosure of certain payments to influence
state legislative or administrative action. (Section 86100, et
seq.) The City of Antioch will incur disclosure obligations under
the Act's lobbying provisions if it qualifies as a lobbyist
employer or it makes payments totaling $5,000 or more in a
calendar quarter to influence legislative or administrative
action. (Section 86115.)

Regular dues or similar payments for membership in a bona
fide association, including any federation, confederation or
trade, labor or membership organization, some portion of which is
used to influence legislative or administrative action, need not
be counted for purposes of determining if an entity qualifies as a
lobbyist employer or $5,000 filer. (Regulation 18616(g)(3).)

Your letter states that the City is a member of the League of
California Cities, but does not indicate whether the City employs
a lobbyist or makes any other types of payments to influence
legislative or administrative action. Therefore, the advice
provided in this letter is limited to payments made by the City
for membership in the League of California Cities. To the extent
that such payments meet the test established in Regulation
18616(g)(3), the city will not be required to file lobbying
disclosure reports.

Enclosed is a copy of the Commission's "Information Manual on
Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act." If
you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
